failure_modes.description: Retrieval ownership evidence is missing, weak, or displaced by downstream grounding symptoms.
symptoms: scope drift selected as citation or grounding failure
failure_modes.safe_action: Add explicit retrieval-stage evidence and targeted tests without changing benchmark labels.
failure_modes.unsafe_action: Raise global retrieval priority or edit golden expected failures to match current output.

failure_modes.description: Claim-to-context evidence no longer preserves unsupported or contradicted claim diagnostics.
failure_modes.safe_action: Strengthen evidence metadata and add narrow regression tests.
failure_modes.unsafe_action: Treat token overlap as final faithfulness or suppress low confidence findings.

failure_modes.description: Citation mismatch or faithfulness evidence is missing, mislabeled, or overruled incorrectly.
failure_modes.safe_action: Make citation evidence explicit and preserve heuristic labels.
failure_modes.unsafe_action: Change citation fixture labels or silently accept missing citations.

failure_modes.description: Insufficient-context detection regresses or leaks domain-specific assumptions.
failure_modes.safe_action: Add domain-agnostic evidence checks and visible degradation metadata.
failure_modes.unsafe_action: Mask missing context as CLEAN or tune thresholds without calibration hooks.

failure_modes.description: Stale retrieval or source-validity evidence is missing or mislabeled.
symptoms: stale retrieval cases become unsupported claims
failure_modes.safe_action: Expose source-date evidence and preserve advisory status where uncalibrated.
failure_modes.unsafe_action: Invent calibrated recency confidence or rewrite version fixtures.

failure_modes.description: Parser or chunking behavior changes damage downstream retrieval evidence.
failure_modes.unsafe_action: Normalize away source boundaries or delete fixture PDFs.

failure_modes.description: Answer-quality signals become opaque or override claim-level evidence.
symptoms: quality score selected as root cause without evidence
failure_modes.safe_action: Keep quality as a signal with explicit evidence status.
failure_modes.unsafe_action: Use answer quality as a diagnosis source of truth.

failure_modes.description: Confidence claims become uncalibrated, hidden, or overstated.
failure_modes.safe_action: Label heuristic confidence and surface degradation metadata.
failure_modes.unsafe_action: Invent confidence scores or mark semantic entropy research-faithful without the mechanism.

failure_modes.description: Security or retrieval anomaly evidence is lost, mislabeled, or downgraded to generic grounding.
symptoms: duplicate retrieved chunks are only warnings
failure_modes.safe_action: Emit structured anomaly evidence with heuristic status.
failure_modes.unsafe_action: Use regex-only prompt injection as a final calibrated security diagnosis.

failure_modes.description: Primary failure selection changes without explicit evidence or targeted tests.
symptoms: structured evidence loses to weak generic findings
failure_modes.safe_action: Add narrow recognition for explicit structured diagnostics with regression tests.
failure_modes.unsafe_action: Change global priorities or expected benchmark outputs.

failure_modes.description: Optional provider calls fail or degrade without visible metadata.
failure_modes.safe_action: Surface provider failures as degraded optional signals.
failure_modes.unsafe_action: Make external providers required for native mode.

failure_modes.description: Uncalibrated heuristics are presented as calibrated production signals.
failure_modes.safe_action: Report readiness gaps and keep production gating disabled.
failure_modes.unsafe_action: Enable production gating or edit readiness reports to hide blockers.

failure_modes.description: Protected benchmark cases, labels, expected stages, or fixtures changed casually.
failure_modes.safe_action: Stop and get explicit user approval before fixture or label changes.
failure_modes.unsafe_action: Edit expected_primary_failure, expected_stage, or expected_fix_category to pass tests.

failure_modes.description: The worktree contains deleted tracked files, unknown generated artifacts, or unrelated dirty state.
failure_modes.safe_action: List dirty state, isolate your patch, and avoid reverting user changes.
failure_modes.unsafe_action: Run destructive cleanup or reset unrelated files.

failure_modes.description: Reports are rewritten to obscure failures, regressions, or degraded providers.
failure_modes.safe_action: Generate additive reports that preserve warnings and command provenance.
failure_modes.unsafe_action: Edit reports to hide Not Ready, degraded runtime, or baseline regressions.
